strengths: 16+ years of software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and microservices at scale
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform demonstrating entrepreneurial initiative
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML product or platform built; recent AI exposure limited to MCP tooling at current role
No evidence of director-level IC influence, strategy advisory, or executive-facing customer engagement
No advanced degree in AI and no cloud-architect-level depth (AWS CDK use is tactical, not strategic)

key_factors.factor: AI expertise and shipped AI products

response.justification: The role demands a director-level IC with recognized AI expertise, shipped AI products, and the gravitas to advise strategic customers on cloud and AI adoption. The candidate meets the 15-year experience bar and shows craftsmanship, full-stack breadth, and entrepreneurial drive via GISCollective, but the résumé cites only recent, tactical AI work ('contribute to AI-agent infrastructure ... MCP code generators') with no evidence of AI products, platforms, research, or thought leadership. Titles peak at Staff/Senior, with no signals of principal-level strategy work, partner/customer advisory, or cloud-architecture depth beyond AWS CDK usage. Strong engineer, but evidence for this specific mandate is thin.
